feedback.text: I strongly support the Digital Fairness Act and urge lawmakers to include clear protections for digital ownership. When consumers purchase digital content - whether it's a game, software, or eBook - they deserve the same rights and expectations as with physical goods. Companies should not be allowed to revoke access to a product post-sale, especially without warning or compensation. This isn't just a gaming issue - it's about basic consumer rights in the digital age. If companies want to sell licenses instead of ownership, they must clearly disclose that before purchase, not after. Fairness, transparency, and respect for digital property are long overdue.
